mnemonics.title: Process States
mnemonics.content: Never Run When Tired (New, Ready, Running, Waiting, Terminated)

mnemonics.title: PCB Components
mnemonics.content: SPRIMACS: State, Program Counter, Registers, I/O Status, Memory, Accounting, CPU Scheduling

mnemonics.title: Scheduling Queues
mnemonics.content: JRD: Job Queue (new processes), Ready Queue (CPU access), Device Queue (I/O wait)

mnemonics.title: Process Operations
mnemonics.content: Create, Terminate, Wait, Load, Execute → Cool Teachers Work Late Evenings

acronyms.usage: Contains all process execution context information
Key process operations in lifecycle management

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular train route: New Station → Ready City → Running Valley → Waiting Town → Terminated Terminal
Picture a relay race - runners pass a baton (CPU) while storing their position (PCB) for next runner
Process as a factory building (resources), threads as assembly lines (execution paths) sharing the space

analogies.analogy: Like airport control tower managing takeoffs (CPU bursts) and ground waits (I/O operations) for multiple planes
Office team working on same project: Shared whiteboard (memory), individual tasks (threads), one conference room (process)
Medical chart in hospital - contains patient's current vitals (state), treatment history (accounting), and test results (I/O status)
